  private static string GetPageCategory(StationpediaPage page)\n        {\n            if (page == null) return \"Other\";\n            \n            // Try to determine category from prefab name or page categories\n            if (page.PageCustomCategories != null &amp;&amp; page.PageCustomCategories.Count &gt; 0)\n            {\n                return page.PageCustomCategories[0];\n            }\n            \n            // Parse category from Key (e.g., \"ThingSomeDevice\" -&gt; check DataHandler)\n            string key = page.Key ?? \"\";\n            \n            // Check against known category lists\n            if (key.StartsWith(\"Thing\"))\n            {\n                string prefabName = key.Substring(5); // Remove \"Thing\" prefix\n                \n                // Try to find in DataHandler's dictionary\n                foreach (var listEntry in Stationpedia.DataHandler._listDictionary)\n                {\n                    foreach (var categoryEntry in listEntry.Value)\n                    {\n                        foreach (var insert in categoryEntry.Value)\n                        {\n                            if (insert.PageLink == key)\n                            {\n                                return categoryEntry.Key; // Return the sub-category name\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            \n            // Check special page types\n            if (key.StartsWith(\"Gas\")) return \"Gases\";\n            if (key.StartsWith(\"Reagent\")) return \"Reagents\";\n            if (key.StartsWith(\"Gene\")) return \"Genetics\";\n            if (key.StartsWith(\"LogicType\")) return \"Logic Variables\";\n            if (key.StartsWith(\"LogicSlotType\")) return \"Logic Slot Variables\";\n            \n            return \"Other\";\n        }\n\n        private static void ReorderSearchUI(Stationpedia stationpedia, List&lt;ScoredResult&gt; scoredResults)\n        {\n            var searchContents = stationpedia.SearchContents;\n            if (searchContents == null) return;\n            \n            // Group by priority, then by category within lower priorities\n            var exactMatches = scoredResults.Where(r =&gt; r.Priority == MatchPriority.ExactTitle).ToList();\n            var startsWithMatches = scoredResults.Where(r =&gt; r.Priority == MatchPriority.TitleStartsWith).ToList();\n            var containsMatches = scoredResults.Where(r =&gt; r.Priority == MatchPriority.TitleContains).ToList();\n            var descriptionMatches = scoredResults.Where(r =&gt; r.Priority == MatchPriority.DescriptionContains).ToList();\n            \n            int siblingIndex = 0;\n            \n            // Add \"Exact Matches\" section\n            if (exactMatches.Count &gt; 0)\n            {\n                siblingIndex = AddCategoryHeader(searchContents, \"🎯 Exact Matches\", siblingIndex);\n AddCategoryHeader(searchContents, \"📌 Title Starts With\", siblingIndex);\n                foreach (var result in startsWithMatches.OrderBy(r =&gt; r.Page.Title))\n                {\n                    SetItemSiblingIndex(searchContents, result, siblingIndex++);\n                }\n            }\n            \n            // For \"Contains\" and \"Description\" matches, group by category\n            var remainingMatches = containsMatches.Concat(descriptionMatches).ToList();\n            if (remainingMatches.Count &gt; 0)\n            {\n                // Group by category\n                var byCategory = remainingMatches\n                    .GroupBy(r =&gt; r.Category)\n                    .OrderBy(g =&gt; g.Key);\n                \n                foreach (var categoryGroup in byCategory)\n                {\n                    string headerText = $\"📁 {categoryGroup.Key}\";\n                    siblingIndex = AddCategoryHeader(searchContents, headerText, siblingIndex);\n                    \n                    foreach (var result in categoryGroup.OrderBy(r =&gt; r.Page.Title))\n                    {\n                        SetItemSiblingIndex(searchContents, result, siblingIndex++);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            \n            // Force layout rebuild\n            LayoutRebuilder.ForceRebuildLayoutImmediate(searchContents);\n        }\n\n        private static int AddCategoryHeader(RectTransform parent, string text, int siblingIndex)\n        {\n            // Create a simple header GameObject\n            var headerGO = new GameObject($\"SearchCategoryHeader_{siblingIndex}\");\n            headerGO.transform.SetParent(parent, false);\n            \n            // Add to tracking list\n            _searchCategoryHeaders.Add(headerGO);\n            \n            // Add RectTransform\n            var rectTransform = headerGO.AddComponent&lt;RectTransform&gt;();\n            rectTransform.anchorMin = new Vector2(0, 1);\n            rectTransform.anchorMax = new Vector2(1, 1);\n            rectTransform.pivot = new Vector2(0.5f, 1);\n            rectTransform.sizeDelta = new Vector2(0, 30);\n            \n            // Add background image\n            var bgImage = headerGO.AddComponent&lt;Image&gt;();\n            bgImage.color = new Color(0.15f, 0.15f, 0.2f, 0.9f);\n            \n            // Add text\n            var textGO = new GameObject(\"HeaderText\");\n            textGO.transform.SetParent(headerGO.transform, false);\n            \n            var textRT = textGO.AddComponent&lt;RectTransform&gt;();\n            textRT.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;\n            textRT.anchorMax = Vector2.one;\n            textRT.offsetMin = new Vector2(10, 0);\n            textRT.offsetMax = new Vector2(-10, 0);\n            \n            var textComponent = textGO.AddComponent&lt;TextMeshProUGUI&gt;();\n            textComponent.text = text;\n            textComponent.fontSize = 14;\n            textComponent.fontStyle = TMPro.FontStyles.Bold;\n            textComponent.color = new Color(1f, 0.5f, 0.1f, 1f); // Orange\n            textComponent.alignment = TextAlignmentOptions.Left;\n            textComponent.verticalAlignment = VerticalAlignmentOptions.Middle;\n            \n            // Try to get font from existing Stationpedia elements\n            try\n            {\n                var existingText = parent.GetComponentInChildren&lt;TextMeshProUGUI&gt;();\n                if (existingText != null)\n                {\n                    textComponent.font = existingText.font;\n                    textComponent.fontSharedMaterial = existingText.fontSharedMaterial;\n                }\n            }\n            catch { }\n            \n            // Add layout element\n            var layoutElement = headerGO.AddComponent&lt;LayoutElement&gt;();\n            layoutElement.preferredHeight = 30;\n            layoutElement.flexibleWidth = 1;\n            \n            // Set sibling index\n            headerGO.transform.SetSiblingIndex(siblingIndex);\n            \n            return siblingIndex + 1;\n        }\n\n        private static void SetItemSiblingIndex(RectTransform parent, ScoredResult result, int targetIndex)\n        {\n            // Find the SPDAListItem that matches this result\n            foreach (Transform child in parent)\n            {\n                var listItem = child.GetComponent&lt;SPDAListItem&gt;();\n                if (listItem == null) continue;\n                \n                string itemTitle = Regex.Replace(listItem.InsertTitle?.text ?? \"\", \"&lt;[^&gt;]+&gt;\", \"\").Trim();\n                string pageTitle = Regex.Replace(result.Page.Title ?? \"\", \"&lt;[^&gt;]+&gt;\", \"\").Trim();\n                \n                if (string.Equals(itemTitle, pageTitle, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))\n                {\n                    child.SetSiblingIndex(targetIndex);\n                    return;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        private static void CleanupCategoryHeaders()\n        {\n            foreach (var header in _searchCategoryHeaders)\n            {\n                if (header != null)\n                {\n                    UnityEngine.Object.Destroy(header);\n                }\n            }\n            _searchCategoryHeaders.Clear();\n            _lastSearchText = \"\";\n        }\n\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\n        /// Reset state for hot-reload support.\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\n        public static void Reset()\n        {\n            CleanupCategoryHeaders();\n        }\n    }\n}\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```

</details>
</tool-use>

TMPro;\r\nusing UnityEngine;\r\nusing UnityEngine.Events;\r\nusing UnityEngine.UI;\r\n\r\nnamespace StationpediaAscended.Patches\r\n{\r\n    /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n    /// Harmony patches for Track created category headers so we can clean them up\r\n        private static List&lt;GameObject&gt; _searchCategoryHeaders = new List&lt;GameObject&gt;();\r\n        \r\n        // Cache the last the search field\r\n        private static bool _searchFieldHooked = false;\r\n        \r\n        // Coroutine reference for debouncing\r\n        private static Coroutine _reorganizeCoroutine = null;\r\n        \r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Priority levels for search result scoring.\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private enum MatchPriority\r\n        {\r\n            ExactTitle = 0,\r\n            TitleStartsWith = 1,\r\n            TitleContains = 2,\r\n            DescriptionContains = 3\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Helper class to store scored search results.\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private class ScoredResult\r\n        {\r\n            public StationpediaPage Page { get; set; }\r\n            public MatchPriority Priority { get; set; }\r\n            public string Category { get; set; }\r\n            public Transform ItemTransform { get; set; }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Postfix patch for ClearPreviousSearch - cleans up our headers.\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        public static void ClearPreviousSearch_Postfix(Stationpedia __instance)\r\n        {\r\n            try\r\n            {\r\n                // Clean up our category headers when search is cleared\r\n                CleanupCategoryHeaders();\r\n                \r\n                // Hook the search field if we haven't already\r\n                if (!_searchFieldHooked)\r\n                {\r\n                    HookSearchField(__instance);\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            catch (Exception ex)\r\n            {\r\n                Debug.LogError($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Error in ClearPreviousSearch_Postfix: {ex.Message}\");\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Hook into the search field's onSubmit to trigger reorganization after search.\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private static void HookSearchField(Stationpedia stationpedia)\r\n        {\r\n            try\r\n            {\r\n                if (stationpedia?.SearchField == null) return;\r\n                \r\n                // Add a listener that will trigger reorganization after a delay\r\n                // This runs after the async search has time to populate results\r\n                stationpedia.SearchField.onSubmit.AddListener(new UnityAction&lt;string&gt;(OnSearchSubmit));\r\n                stationpedia.SearchField.onValueChanged.AddListener(new UnityAction&lt;string&gt;(OnSearchValueChanged));\r\n                \r\n                _searchFieldHooked = true;\r\n                ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Search field hooked for result reorganization\");\r\n            }\r\n            catch (Exception ex)\r\n            {\r\n                Debug.LogError($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Error hooking search field: {ex.Message}\");\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        private static void OnSearchSubmit(string searchText)\r\n        {\r\n            if (StationpediaAscendedMod.Instance != null)\r\n            {\r\n                // Stop any pending reorganization\r\n                if (_reorganizeCoroutine != null)\r\n                {\r\n                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Instance.StopCoroutine(_reorganizeCoroutine);\r\n                }\r\n                // Start new one with short delay for async search to complete\r\n                _reorganizeCoroutine = StationpediaAscendedMod.Instance.StartCoroutine(\r\n                    DelayedReorganize(searchText, 0.3f));\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        private static void OnSearchValueChanged(string searchText)\r\n        {\r\n            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(searchText))\r\n            {\r\n                CleanupCategoryHeaders();\r\n                return;\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            if (StationpediaAscendedMod.Instance != null &amp;&amp; searchText.Length &gt;= 3)\r\n            {\r\n                // Stop any pending }\r\n                // Start new one with longer delay for typing\r\n                _reorganizeCoroutine = StationpediaAscendedMod.Instance.StartCoroutine(\r\n                    DelayedReorganize(searchText, 0.8f));\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        private static IEnumerator DelayedReorganize(string searchText, float delay)\r\n        {\r\n            yield return new WaitForSeconds(delay);\r\n            \r\n            // Wait for search to finish populating (check for stable result count)\r\n            int lastCount = -1;\r\n            for \r\n                int currentCount = CountVisibleSearchResults(stationpedia.SearchContents);\r\n                if (currentCount &gt; 0 &amp;&amp; currentCount == lastCount)\r\n                {\r\n \"\";\r\n                    var page = FindPageByTitle(title);\r\n                    \r\n                    if (page != null)\r\n                    {\r\n                        items.Add((listItem, page, child));\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                if (items.Count == 0) return;\r\n                \r\n                ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Reorganizing {items.Count} search results for '{searchText}'\");\r\n                \r\n                // Score and categorize results\r\n                var scoredResults = ScoreResults(items, searchText);\r\n                \r\n                // Reorganize the UI\r\n                ReorderSearchUI(stationpedia, scoredResults);\r\n            }\r\n            catch (Exception ex)\r\n            {\r\n                Debug.LogError($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Error in ReorganizeSearchResults: {ex.Message}\\n{ex.StackTrac\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",

  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\nTransform transform)&gt; items,\r\n            string searchText)\r\n        {\r\n            var results = new List&lt;ScoredResult&gt;();\r\n            string searchLower = searchText.ToLowerInvariant();\r\n            \r\n            foreach (var (item, page, transform) in items)\r\n            {\r\n                string title = Regex.Replace(page.Title ?? \"\", \"&lt;[^&gt;]+&gt;\", \"\").ToLowerInvariant();\r\n                \r\n                MatchPriority priority;\r\n                \r\n                if (title == searchLower)\r\n                {\r\n                    priority = MatchPriority.ExactTitle;\r\n                }\r\n                else if (title.StartsWith(searchLower))\r\n                {\r\n                    priority = MatchPriority.TitleStartsWith;\r\n                }\r\n                else if (title.Contains(searchLower))\r\n                {\r\n                    priority = MatchPriority.TitleContains;\r\n                }\r\n                else\r\n                {\r\n                    priority = MatchPriority.DescriptionContains;\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Get category from page\r\n                string category = GetPageCategory(page);\r\n                \r\n                results.Add(new ScoredResult\r\n                {\r\n                    Page = page,\r\n                    Priority = priority,\r\n                    Category = category,\r\n                    ItemTransform = transform\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",

The user wants me to try implementing the full categorized search results feature. Let me dive in and see how quickly I can get this working.

I need to:
1. Create a Harmony patch that intercepts the search results
2. Score/categorize results by: exact match, starts with, then by category
3. Modify the UI to show category headers

Let me start by looking at the existing Patches.cs to see the pattern, then add the new search enhancement.Let's do it! I'll start implementing right away. Now let me create the search enhancement patch. I'll add it as a new file: Now I need to register these patches in the main mod file and find the right hook point. Let me check the mod's initialization: Now I need to add the search patches to the ApplyHarmonyPatches method. Let me find a good hook - I'll use ClearPreviousSearch which gets called before each search, and I'll hook into the search field's onValueChanged to trigger reorganization after results are populated: I need to add the search patches. The tricky part is that `DoSearch` is async. Let me think of a better approach - I'll hook into `ClearPreviousSearch` and also create a coroutine that triggers after search results settle: Now I need to add the using directive for SearchPatches. Let me find the usings section: Good, `StationpediaAscended.Patches` is already imported. Now I need to figure out how to trigger the reorganization. The problem is that `DoSearch` is async. I'll add a coroutine-based approach that listens for changes. Let me update the SearchPatches to be more robust: Now let me update the rest of the file to fix the main reorganization method: Now update the ScoreResults to use the new tuple type: Now update the ReorderSearchUI to use ItemTransform directly: Now remove the old SetItemSiblingIndex method and update Reset: Now let's build and see if there are any errors: It builds. Now let me check if there are any issues with the DataHandler access - that might be an internal/private field: Good, `_listDictionary` is public. Now let me also add the static patching call in the static version. Let me check if there's a static patching method: I need to add the search patch to the static path as well. Let me add it: Now let me copy to the scripts folder and test: The mod is deployed. You can test it now in-game:

1. Launch Stationeers
2. Press F6 to reload scripts (or restart game)
3. Open Stationpedia (F1)
4. Search for "corn" and see if it reorganizes with headers

The implementation:
- **🎯 Exact Matches** - Items where title matches exactly
- **📌 Title Starts With** - Items where title begins with search term  
- **📁 [Category Name]** - Remaining items grouped by their Stationpedia category

Let me know how it works! If there are issues I can iterate quickly. The main risk points are:
1. The coroutine timing for when search results are "done"
2. Font matching for the category headers
3. Category detection from DataHandler

---

Looking at the screenshot, I can see:
1. The categories are working! "Cables and Analyzers" and "Other" are showing
2. But there's a corrupted character (looks like a box/square) in front of the category names
3. No "Exact Matches" or "Title Starts With" sections are showing - which means the search term "corn" isn't matching any titles exactly or at the start

The corrupted characters are likely the emoji (🎯, 📌, 📁) - the game's font probably doesn't support those Unicode characters.

Let me fix:
1. Replace emojis with ASCII-safe alternatives
2. Check why exact matches aren't working - maybe the title comparison is off

For "corn" - there should be actual Corn items (the plant/food). Let me check if the issue is that the search is finding "Corner" in things but no items literally called "Corn" are being matched.

Actually wait - if searching "corn" and there ARE corn plants/seeds in the game, they should show as exact matches. Maybe the issue is case sensitivity or the page title stripping.I see the issues! The emoji characters aren't rendering (showing as boxes), and exact matches aren't appearing. Let me fix both: Now let me also add some debug logging to understand why exact matches aren't working: Done! Press F6 to reload and try again. The changes:

1. **Replaced emojis with ASCII** - `>>` for exact, `>` for starts-with, `-` for categories
2. **Added debug logging** - Check the console (F3) to see what's being matched
3. **Added `.Trim()`** - In case there's whitespace issues

For "corn" specifically - try searching and look at the console output. It will show any exact or starts-with matches it finds. If there are actual "Corn" items in the game (plant/seed), they should now show in the `>> Exact Matches` section.

2. The Corn page doesn't exist
3. There's something else going on

```

So if "Corn" exists as a page with Title "Corn", the regex "corn" should match it. Unless...

Actually, I think the real issue might be that "Corn" as a food item might be paginated out or the vanilla search has issues. But we can't fix what the vanilla search returns.

The critical issue remains that zero food-related items are surfacing in the current 40-result set, suggesting a deeper indexing or search algorithm problem beyond simple matching techniques.

1. Exact title match
2. Title starts with search term

This strategy ensures the most relevant results appear first, solving the core challenge of surfacing specific content like the Corn food page.

The current search algorithm incorrectly elevates partial word matches, so we'll implement word boundary detection to distinguish between true matches and incidental text overlaps. By creating a hierarchical scoring system, we can dramatically improve search precision.

The log reveals vanilla search is fundamentally flawed, returning items like "Wall (Padded No Border Corner)" simply because they contain a partial text match. Our proposed solution will filter these less relevant results, pushing whole-word matches to the top of search results.

I'll use a regex approach to definitively identify genuine word-level matches. The vanilla search finds "corn" inside "corner" but apparently "Corn" the food item either doesn't exist as a page or has a different internal name.

However, we CAN improve our scoring to use **word boundary matching** - so if "Corn" WAS in the results, it would be prioritized over "Corner" items. Let me update the scoring logic: Now the scoring uses **word boundary matching**:
- **ExactTitle**: "corn" matches "Corn" exactly
- **TitleStartsWith**: "corn seed" starts with the word "corn"  
- **TitleContains (word)**: "popped corn" contains the word "corn"
- **DescriptionContains (demoted)**: "corner" contains "corn" but only as part of another word

This means all the "Corner" items will now be demoted to the lowest priority. If "Corn" the food exists and is returned by vanilla search, it would show first.

This is really strange - the user is saying that "corn" specifically doesn't work while other words do. Let me think about what could be happening...

The vanilla search is returning 40 results for "corn" but they're all "corner" items. The actual food item "Corn" isn't being returned by the vanilla search at all.

This could be because:
1. The Corn food item doesn't have a Stationpedia page
2. The Corn item is named something else (like "ItemCorn" internally)
3. There's something special about how the search works

Let me check the Assembly-CSharp code to see if there are any corn-related items and how they might be named. I should search for "corn" in the codebase to understand what the actual item name is.That's bizarre! Let me search the game code to see what "Corn" is actually called internally - maybe it has a different name in the Stationpedia: I see there's a `CookedCorn.cs` and `Reagents\Corn.cs`. Let me check what items exist with "Corn" in their name: Let me look at the Stationpedia code to see how pages are generated - maybe corn is in a "Seeds" category or has a different title: Let me look at how the search actually searches - it matches against what fields: Let me look at the DoSearch method to understand what it actually searches against: Let me look at how the async DoSearch method processes pages: Let me look for how the actual DoSearch method works - it's probably an async state machine. Let me search for how pages are matched: I see the issue now! The vanilla search builds a pattern like `(?=.*corn)` which uses lookahead to match "corn" anywhere in the string. This matches "corner" because it contains "corn". The vanilla search just matches substring - there's no word-boundary handling.

But the weird thing is - there should be a "Corn" item in the game. Let me search for how food items are named: There's a `CookedCorn` but what about raw corn? In Stationeers, the "Corn" item is likely a plant/seed or the raw version. Let me check what the in-game names are: So seeds are derived from plants. The issue is likely that in-game the Corn plant/seed just might not have "Corn" in its *display name* (maybe it's named differently, like "Sweet Corn" or uses a different localization). 

The vanilla search actually seems to be working correctly - it's finding items that contain "corn" (in "corner"). If the game doesn't have an item that's displayed as just "Corn" or "Corn Seed", then it won't appear.

Let me check what other search terms work to understand the pattern better. Let me update the debug logging to dump ALL titles the search finds:

   {\r\n            var missingPages = new List&lt;StationpediaPage&gt;();\r\n            string searchLower = searchText.ToLowerInvariant().Trim();\r\n            string escapedSearch = Regex.Escape(searchLower);\r\n            Regex wholeWordRegex = new Regex($@\"\\b{escapedSearch}\\b\", RegexOptions.IgnoreCase);\r\n            \r\n            foreach (var page in Stationpedia.StationpediaPages)\r\n            {\r\n                // Skip if already in results\r\n                if (existingPageKeys.Contains(page.Key)) continue;\r\n                \r\n priority items only)\r\n                bool isExact = title == searchLower;\r\n                bool isWholeWord = wholeWordRegex.IsMatch(title);\r\n                \r\n                if (isExact || isWholeWord)\r\n                {\r\n                    ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[Search] INJECTING missing match: '{page.Title}' (exact: {isExact}, wholeWord: {isWholeWord})\");\r\n                    missingPages.Add(page);\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            return missingPages;\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Create UI elements for List&lt;StationpediaPage&gt; pages,\r\n            List&lt;(SPDAListItem item, StationpediaPage page, Transform transform)&gt; items,\r\n            HashSet&lt;string&gt; existingPageKeys)\r\n This is fascinating! Looking at the logs:

1. "tomato" - works perfectly: finds "Tomato", "Tomato Soup", "Tomato Seeds", "Cooked Tomato"
2. "water" - works: finds "Water", "Water Bottle", etc.
3. "carbon" - works: finds "Carbon", "Carbon Dioxide", etc.
4. "cooked" - works: finds "Cooked Rice", "Cooked Corn", "Cooked Mushroom", etc.

But "corn" returns 39-42 results and they're ALL "corner" items - the actual Corn food isn't showing up!

BUT WAIT - look at this line:
```
01:16:24: [Stationpedia Ascended] Reorganizing 3 search results for 'itemcorn'
01:16:24: [Search] Item: 'corn' | wholeWord: False
01:16:24: [Search] Item: 'corn seeds' | wholeWord: False
01:16:24: [Search] Item: 'corn soup' | wholeWord: False
```

When searching "itemcorn", the vanilla search DOES find "Corn", "Corn Seeds", and "Corn Soup"! But searching just "corn" doesn't find them.

This is a vanilla game search bug! The vanilla search has the 40 "corner" items match first and then hits some limit, so it never gets to the actual "Corn" items.

The key insight: The vanilla search finds matches in order of how pages are stored, not alphabetically or by relevance. There are so many "corner" items that they fill up the results before "Corn" items are reached.

This is actually not something we can fix with reorganization - we need to either:
1. Modify the vanilla search to have a higher limit
2. Do our own search that searches ALL pages and adds missing relevant items

Let me check the vanilla search code to see if there's a limit we can adjust...


I see a potential workaround by examining the Stationpedia search method. We could implement a post-search filtering mechanism that ensures exact matches are prioritized. By searching through the full page list and injecting precise results, we can overcome the current limitation of the vanilla search algorithm. This approach would dynamically supplement the initial search results with missing but relevant items.This is a **vanilla game bug**! Look at these key lines:

- `'itemcorn'` → finds "corn", "corn seeds", "corn soup" (3 results)
- `'corn'` → finds 42 "corner" items, but NO corn food items!

The vanilla search has a limit (probably 40-50 results) and returns matches in the order pages are stored. There are SO many "corner" items that they fill up the results before the actual "Corn" items are reached.

The fix: We need to **inject missing exact/whole-word matches** that the vanilla search missed. Let me add that: Now I'll add the feature to **inject missing exact/whole-word matches** that the vanilla search missed: Now I need to add the tracking list and update cleanup: Now let's build and test: Let me check the SPDAListItem class for the correct property name: I see - SPDAListItem doesn't store a page reference directly. Instead, the button click is set up elsewhere. Let me check how Stationpedia adds items to the search results: Let me see how search results are set up with their buttons: I see - the button click handlers are set via `AddListener` with a closure. Let me update the injection code: Build succeeded. Now test searching for "corn" - it should:

1. **Inject** the missing "Corn", "Corn Seeds", "Corn Soup", "Cooked Corn" items that were hidden by all the "corner" results
2. **Sort them at the top** as exact/whole-word matches
3. **Demote** all the "corner" items to the bottom

The console should show:
```
[Search] INJECTING missing match: 'Corn' (exact: true, wholeWord: true)
[Search] INJECTING missing match: 'Corn Seeds' (exact: false, wholeWord: true)
...
```

---

The user is asking why the CustomSpriteToUse is null for food items like Corn. Looking at the vanilla code earlier, I saw that sprites come from different sources:

1. `prefab.ButtonImage.sprite` for Thing pages
2. `ImportantSearchImage` as fallback
3. Various specific images for LogicTypes, Reagents, Genes, etc.

For food items/plants, the sprite should come from the prefab. Let me check how the vanilla search does it - it must be populating the sprites somehow since the items show correctly when found by vanilla search.

Looking back at the log, when searching "itemcorn", vanilla DID find "corn", "corn seeds", "corn soup" - and those would have had correct sprites. So the pages DO have sprites when loaded via vanilla search.

The issue is that `page.CustomSpriteToUse` might be lazily loaded or populated only when the search actually finds it. Or maybe the sprite is associated with the search result item differently.

Let me think... when vanilla search finds a result, it instantiates an SPDAListItem and sets its image from the page. So the page SHOULD have the sprite already populated.

Wait - in the screenshot from the first successful test, the first "Corn" HAD an icon (the beaker thing), but the second "Corn" didn't. That first one was probably from vanilla search, and the second was our injected one.

So the pages DO have `CustomSpriteToUse` set - but maybe there are multiple pages with the same title? Let me check if there could be duplicate pages...

Actually, looking at it again - the first Corn with the icon might be from vanilla results (vanilla DID return some corn items when there were fewer "corner" items), and our injected ones had null sprites.

The real question is: why would `page.CustomSpriteToUse` be null for some corn pages but not others?

Looking at the Stationpedia code:
- Line 792: `page.CustomSpriteToUse = prefab.ButtonImage.sprite;` - this is set during population


- Line 804-806: If it's still null after that, it uses `ImportantSearchImage` as fallback

I suspect the core issue involves page identification. When searching for food items, we might be matching different page variants - seed pages, food item pages - which could explain the inconsistent sprite assignment. The fallback mechanism exists, but something is preventing its proper execution for our injected search results.

A potential solution involves directly extracting sprite references from the prefab, bypassing the current page lookup complexity. This approach could provide a more reliable image retrieval method.The sprites for "Thing" pages (items, structures, etc.) are loaded from the actual prefab's `ButtonImage.sprite` during Stationpedia initialization. When we inject pages, the `CustomSpriteToUse` might not be populated yet for some pages, or there could be duplicate pages (like both a plant and food item named "Corn").

Let me add a fallback to use Stationpedia's default images when the sprite is null: Good - `ImportantSearchImage` is the fallback. Let me update the injection code to use that: Now injected items will show `ImportantSearchImage` (a generic icon) when the page's sprite is null.

As for **why** sprites are null: Food items like "Corn" are probably created as `Item` prefabs rather than `Thing` prefabs. The sprite loading code (`page.CustomSpriteToUse = prefab.ButtonImage.sprite`) runs during `PopulateThingPages()` but the prefab reference might not exist for dynamically created items, or the Item/Food pages might be populated through a different code path that doesn't set the sprite.

Test again and you should see at least a default icon now instead of blank.

---

{
  "invocationMessage": "Using \"Replace String in File\"",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\n/// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Try to load the sprite/thumbnail from the prefab based on page key.\r\n        /// Page keys are like \"ThingItemCorn\" or \"ItemCorn\" for items.\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private static Sprite TryGetSpriteFromPrefab(string pageKey)\r\n        {\r\n            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(pageKey)) return null;\r\n            \r\n            try\r\n            {\r\n                string prefabName = null;\r\n                \r\n                // Extract prefab name from page key\r\n                if (pageKey.StartsWith(\"Thing\"))\r\n                {\r\n                    prefabName = pageKey.Substring(5); // Remove \"Thing\" prefix\r\n                }\r\n                else if (pageKey.StartsWith(\"Item\"))\r\n                {\r\n                    prefabName = pageKey; // Items use their full name\r\n                }\r\n                else\r\n                {\r\n                    prefabName = pageKey;\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Try to find the prefab\r\n                Thing thing = Prefab.Find(prefabName);\r\n                if (thing != null)\r\n                {\r\n                    return thing.GetThumbnail();\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Try with \"Item\" prefix if not found\r\n                if (!prefabName.StartsWith(\"Item\"))\r\n                {\r\n                    thing = Prefab.Find(\"Item\" + prefabName);\r\n                    if (thing != null)\r\n                    {\r\n                        return thing.GetThumbnail();\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            catch (Exception ex)\r\n            {\r\n                Debug.LogWarning($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Could not load sprite for {pageKey}: {ex.Message}\");\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            return null;\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        \n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "/// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Try to load the sprite/thumbnail from the prefab based on page key.\r\n        /// Page keys are like \"ThingItemCorn\" or \"ItemCorn\" for items.\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private static Sprite TryGetSpriteFromPrefab(string pageKey)\r\n        {\r\n            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(pageKey)) return null;\r\n            \r\n            try\r\n            {\r\n                string prefabName = null;\r\n                \r\n                // Extract prefab name from page key\r\n                if (pageKey.StartsWith(\"Thing\"))\r\n                {\r\n                    prefabName = pageKey.Substring(5); // Remove \"Thing\" prefix\r\n                }\r\n                else if (pageKey.StartsWith(\"Item\"))\r\n                {\r\n                    prefabName = pageKey; // Items use their full name\r\n                }\r\n                else\r\n                {\r\n                    prefabName = pageKey;\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Try to find the prefab\r\n                Thing thing = Prefab.Find(prefabName);\r\n                if (thing != null)\r\n                {\r\n                    return thing.GetThumbnail();\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Try with \"Item\" prefix if not found\r\n                if (!prefabName.StartsWith(\"Item\"))\r\n                {\r\n                    thing = Prefab.Find(\"Item\" + prefabName);\r\n                    if (thing != null)\r\n                    {\r\n                        return thing.GetThumbnail();\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            catch (Exception ex)\r\n            {\r\n                Debug.LogWarning($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Could not load sprite for {pageKey}: {ex.Message}\");\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            return null;\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        ",
  "toolCallResult": null
}

Looking at the Stationpedia code, the sprites are loaded from prefabs during initialization. For "Thing" pages, they get the sprite from `prefab.ButtonImage.sprite`. 

I need to figure out how to get the actual sprite for items like "Corn". Let me look at how the game loads these sprites and see if we can manually fetch them.

The key code from Stationpedia.cs:
```csharp
page.CustomSpriteToUse = prefab.ButtonImage.sprite;
```

This happens during `PopulateThingPages()`. The prefab is fetched from `Prefab.Find()` or similar.

For items/food, the page key is like "ItemCorn" or "ThingCorn". Let me check what pattern they use. Looking at the search results, when searching "itemcorn" it found "corn", "corn seeds", "corn soup" - so those are the Item pages.

I should try to load the sprite from the actual prefab if `CustomSpriteToUse` is null. The prefab name can be extracted from the page key.

Let me look for how to get sprites from prefabs in this codebase.Let me look at how sprites are loaded from prefabs so we can do the same for injected items: I see - they use `Prefab.Find()` and `GetThumbnail()`. Let me look at how the page Key relates to prefab names: I see the pattern: `"Thing" + prefab.PrefabName` is the page key. And we can use `thing.GetThumbnail()` to get the sprite. Let me update the injection code to try loading the sprite from the prefab: Now let me update the sprite loading logic: Now add the helper method to fetch sprite from prefab: Now injected items will try to load their actual thumbnail from the prefab using `Prefab.Find()` and `GetThumbnail()`. This is the same approach the game uses internally when creating page entries.

Test "corn" again - the injected Corn, Corn Seeds, Corn Soup items should now have their actual thumbnails.

{

  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1

\"\";\r\n            string title = page.Title ?? \"\";\r\n            \r\n            // Hide ruptured/burnt cables\r\n            if (key.Contains(\"Ruptured\") || title.Contains(\"Ruptured\"))\r\n                return true;\r\n            if (key.Contains(\"CableRuptured\"))\r\n                return true;\r\n                \r\n            // Hide burnt items\r\n            if (title.StartsWith(\"Burnt \") || key.Contains(\"Burnt\"))\r\n                return true;\r\n                \r\n            return false;\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Fix missing sprites for search result items.\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private static void FixMissingSprite(SPDAListItem listItem, StationpediaPage page, Stationpedia stationpedia)\r\n        {\r\n            if (listItem?.InsertImage == null) return;\r\n            \r\n            // Only fix if sprite is missing\r\n            if (listItem.InsertImage.sprite != null) return;\r\n            \r\n            Sprite sprite = page.CustomSpriteToUse;\r\n            \r\n            // If no sprite on page, try to load from prefab\r\n            if (sprite == null)\r\n            {\r\n                sprite = TryGetSpriteFromPrefab(page.Key);\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            // Last resort: use Stationpedia's fallback image  \r\n            if (sprite == null)\r\n            {\r\n                sprite = stationpedia.ImportantSearchImage;\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            listItem.InsertImage.sprite = sprite;\r\n            listItem.InsertImage.gameObject.SetActive(sprite != null);\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        \n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",

The issue is that search results display items from `_SPDASearchInserts`, and sprites come from `page.CustomSpriteToUse`.    - Titles containing "Ruptured"
   - Titles starting with "Burnt " or keys containing "Burnt"

The burnt cables are still showing up. Looking at the screenshot, I can see items like "Burnt Super-Heavy Cable (Corner)" and "Burnt Cable (Corner)" are still appearing. 

The issue is that my filter checks for `title.StartsWith("Burnt ")` but the FindPageByTitle function returns a page based on the title, and I'm checking the page's title, not the display title from the UI. Let me check the flow:

1. We get the listItem with its display title
2. We find the page by that title
3. We check if the page should be hidden

The problem is that `ShouldHideFromSearch` checks `page.Title` but maybe the title format is different, or the check isn't working correctly. Let me look at the titles in the screenshot:

- "Burnt Super-Heavy Cable (Corner)"
- "Burnt Super-Heavy Cable (Corner 4)"
- "Burnt Super-Heavy Cable (Corner 3)"
- "Burnt Heavy Cable (4-Way Corner)"
- "Burnt Cable (Corner)"
- "Burnt Cable (4-Way Corner)"
- "Burnt Cable (3-Way Corner)"

All of these start with "Burnt " so the check `title.StartsWith("Burnt ")` should work. But wait - maybe the title has rich text tags that I'm not stripping? Let me update the filter to check the raw title as well as strip any tags.

Actually, looking at my code again:
```csharp
private static bool ShouldHideFromSearch(StationpediaPage page)
{
    if (page == null) return false;
    
    string key = page.Key ?? "";
    string title = page.Title ?? "";
    
    // Hide ruptured/burnt cables
    if (key.Contains("Ruptured") || title.Contains("Ruptured"))
        return true;
    if (key.Contains("CableRuptured"))
        return true;
        
    // Hide burnt items
    if (title.StartsWith("Burnt ") || key.Contains("Burnt"))
        return true;
        
    return false;
}
```

I'll modify the code to strip HTML tags from the title before checking. This should resolve the issue with rich text formatting preventing the filter from working correctly.The issue is that `page.Title` might have rich text tags (like `<b>` tags) that prevent the `StartsWith` check from working. Let me fix the filter to strip tags first: Fixed - now the filter strips rich text tags (like `<b>`) before checking for "Burnt". Test the search again and those burnt cables should be gone.

The issue is clear now - the burnt cables are being INJECTED by the `FindMissingMatches` function! The filter only runs on items from the vanilla search, but we're injecting burnt items as "missing matches" because they contain the word "burnt" as a whole word.

I need to also apply the filter in `FindMissingMatches` before adding pages to the injection list.The problem is that burnt cables are being **injected** by `FindMissingMatches`! The filter only runs on vanilla search results, not on the pages we inject. Let me fix that: Fixed! Now `ShouldHideFromSearch` is also checked before injecting missing matches. Burnt cables won't be injected anymore, even when you search for "burnt".

The user is saying burnt cables still show up for "corn" search. This means vanilla search is returning them, and our filter IS running but maybe not catching them all. Let me search for how Stationpedia filters or excludes certain items.Let me search for how Stationpedia filters items: Found it! `HiddenInPedia` is the dictionary that controls which items are hidden. Let me see how it's used: I see - there's `thing.HideInStationpedia` and also `HiddenInPedia` dictionary. Let me check what HiddenInPedia contains: Let me check what SPDADataHandler does: So the game has a `HiddenInPedia` dictionary and `HideInStationpedia` property on `Thing`. The burnt cables might have these set but they're still showing up in search because the search doesn't check these flags. Let me update our filter to use the prefab's `HideInStationpedia` flag: Updated! Now the filter:

1. **Checks `thing.HideInStationpedia`** - Uses the same flag the game uses on prefabs
2. **Checks `HiddenInPedia` dictionary** - Respects any data-driven hiding from SPDADataHandler
3. **Filters "burnt" items** - Title or key matching
4. **Filters "ruptured" items** - Title or key matching
5. **Filters "wreckage" items** - Title or key matching

Test "corn" again - burnt cables should now be filtered out properly since they likely have `HideInStationpedia = true` on the prefab.

  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\nHidden Items Population\r\n        \r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Add burnt cables, wreckage, and other debris items to HiddenInPedia \r\n        /// so vanilla search doesn't return them at all.\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private static void PopulateHiddenItems()\r\n        {\r\n            if (_hiddenItemsPopulated) return;\r\n            _hiddenItemsPopulated = true;\r\n            \r\n            try\r\n            {\r\n                var hiddenPedia = Stationpedia.DataHandler?.HiddenInPedia;\r\n                if (hiddenPedia == null)\r\n                {\r\n                    Log?.LogWarning(\"HiddenInPedia dictionary not available\");\r\n                    return;\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                int hiddenCount = 0;\r\n                \r\n                // Iterate through all prefabs and hide unwanted items\r\n                foreach (Thing thing in Prefab.AllPrefabs)\r\n                {\r\n                    if (thing == null) continue;\r\n                    \r\n                    string prefabName = thing.PrefabName ?? \"\";\r\n                    string displayName = thing.DisplayName ?? \"\";\r\n                    string displayNameLower = displayName.ToLowerInvariant();\r\n                    string prefabNameLower = prefabName.ToLowerInvariant();\r\n                    \r\n                    bool shouldHide = false;\r\n                    \r\n                    // Hide burnt items\r\n                    if (displayNameLower.StartsWith(\"burnt\") || prefabNameLower.Contains(\"burnt\"))\r\n                        shouldHide = true;\r\n                    \r\n                    // Hide ruptured items\r\n                    if (displayNameLower.Contains(\"ruptured\") || prefabNameLower.Contains(\"ruptured\"))\r\n                        shouldHide = true;\r\n                    \r\n                    // Hide CableRuptured class items\r\n                    if (thing is Assets.Scripts.Objects.Electrical.CableRuptured)\r\n                        shouldHide = true;\r\n                    \r\n                    // Hide wreckage items\r\n                    if (displayNameLower.Contains(\"wreckage\") || prefabNameLower.Contains(\"wreckage\"))\r\n                        shouldHide = true;\r\n                    \r\n                    if (shouldHide &amp;&amp; !hiddenPedia.ContainsKey(prefabName))\r\n                    {\r\n                        hiddenPedia[prefabName] = true;\r\n                        thing.HideInStationpedia = true;\r\n                        hiddenCount++;\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Hidden {hiddenCount} debris items from search (burnt cables, wreckage, etc.)\");\r\n            }\r\n            catch (Exception ex)\r\n            {\r\n                Log?.LogError($\"Error populating hidden items: {ex.Message}\");\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        #endregion\r\n\r\n        #region \n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "Hidden Items Population\r\n        \r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Add burnt cables, wreckage, and other debris items to HiddenInPedia \r\n        /// so vanilla search doesn't return them at all.\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private static void PopulateHiddenItems()\r\n        {\r\n            if (_hiddenItemsPopulated) return;\r\n            _hiddenItemsPopulated = true;\r\n            \r\n            try\r\n            {\r\n                var hiddenPedia = Stationpedia.DataHandler?.HiddenInPedia;\r\n                if (hiddenPedia == null)\r\n                {\r\n                    Log?.LogWarning(\"HiddenInPedia dictionary not available\");\r\n                    return;\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                int hiddenCount = 0;\r\n                \r\n                // Iterate through all prefabs and hide unwanted items\r\n                foreach (Thing thing in Prefab.AllPrefabs)\r\n                {\r\n                    if (thing == null) continue;\r\n                    \r\n                    string prefabName = thing.PrefabName ?? \"\";\r\n                    string displayName = thing.DisplayName ?? \"\";\r\n                    string displayNameLower = displayName.ToLowerInvariant();\r\n                    string prefabNameLower = prefabName.ToLowerInvariant();\r\n                    \r\n                    bool shouldHide = false;\r\n                    \r\n                    // Hide burnt items\r\n                    if (displayNameLower.StartsWith(\"burnt\") || prefabNameLower.Contains(\"burnt\"))\r\n                        shouldHide = true;\r\n                    \r\n                    // Hide ruptured items\r\n                    if (displayNameLower.Contains(\"ruptured\") || prefabNameLower.Contains(\"ruptured\"))\r\n                        shouldHide = true;\r\n                    \r\n                    // Hide CableRuptured class items\r\n                    if (thing is Assets.Scripts.Objects.Electrical.CableRuptured)\r\n                        shouldHide = true;\r\n                    \r\n                    // Hide wreckage items\r\n                    if (displayNameLower.Contains(\"wreckage\") || prefabNameLower.Contains(\"wreckage\"))\r\n                        shouldHide = true;\r\n                    \r\n                    if (shouldHide &amp;&amp; !hiddenPedia.ContainsKey(prefabName))\r\n                    {\r\n                        hiddenPedia[prefabName] = true;\r\n                        thing.HideInStationpedia = true;\r\n                        hiddenCount++;\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[Stationpedia Ascended] Hidden {hiddenCount} debris items from search (burnt cables, wreckage, etc.)\");\r\n            }\r\n            catch (Exception ex)\r\n            {\r\n                Log?.LogError($\"Error populating hidden items: {ex.Message}\");\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        #endregion\r\n\r\n        #region ",

}

The user is saying:
1. Burnt items show up initially when searching, then disappear (our filter is working but only after the delay)
2. When searching "corn" or "cable", burnt items still show up way later in the list
3. ItemWreckageSteelCornerSmall still shows up

The issue is that our filter only runs AFTER the reorganization delay, but the vanilla search has already populated and displayed results. We're filtering items from our reorganization, but vanilla search results are still visible until we process them.

Also, our filter isn't catching items that were returned by vanilla search - we're only filtering during:
1. Injection (FindMissingMatches) - this works
2. Collection phase in ReorganizeSearchResults - but we're setting itemsToHide and calling SetActive(false), which should work...

Wait, the issue might be that the vanilla search results are displayed immediately, and our reorganization runs after a delay. So users see the unfiltered results first.

But more importantly, the user is asking: can we ADD these items to the HiddenInPedia dictionary so they're filtered at the source?

Let me check if we can modify HiddenInPedia. Since it's just a Dictionary<string, bool>, we could add entries to it during mod initialization. This would make the game's own search filter them out before we even see them!

This is a much better solution - we'd add burnt cables, wreckage, etc. to HiddenInPedia on mod load, and then the vanilla search wouldn't return them at all.

Let me implement this approach.You're right - the issue is our filter only runs during reorganization (after a delay), but vanilla search has already displayed results. The better solution is to **add these items to `HiddenInPedia` at mod startup** so vanilla search never returns them in the first place! Now I understand the structure. I need to add a method that populates the HiddenInPedia dictionary during mod startup. The best place would be in the Initialize() method, after Stationpedia is available.

Actually, looking at the code flow, HiddenInPedia is accessed from Stationpedia.DataHandler.HiddenInPedia. We can add entries to it after Stationpedia is initialized.

The tricky part is that we need to do this AFTER Stationpedia is loaded but BEFORE any search happens. Looking at the code:
1. `Initialize()` is called from `Awake()`
2. The monitor coroutine checks for `Stationpedia.Instance`

We could add a one-time initialization in the monitor coroutine that populates HiddenInPedia when Stationpedia is first found.

Let me add a method to populate HiddenInPedia with all burnt cables, wreckage, etc.Now I see the structure. Let me add a method that populates `HiddenInPedia` with burnt/wreckage items when Stationpedia is found. This way the vanilla search will never return them: Now add the method to hide unwanted items and call it when Stationpedia is found: Now add the method to populate HiddenInPedia and call it when Stationpedia is found: Now add the actual method to populate hidden items. Let me find a good place to add it: Also add the call for the static ScriptEngine path: Now build and deploy: Now the mod will:

1. **At startup** (when Stationpedia is first found): Iterate through ALL prefabs and add burnt cables, wreckage, and ruptured items to `HiddenInPedia` dictionary
2. This happens **before** you ever search, so vanilla search will **never return these items**
3. Sets both `HiddenInPedia[prefabName] = true` AND `thing.HideInStationpedia = true` for full coverage

You should see a console message like:
```
[Stationpedia Ascended] Hidden XX debris items from search (burnt cables, wreckage, etc.)
```

Test "corn" now - burnt cables should be completely gone from the start!

The user is showing me the console output from testing. Let me analyze what's happening:

1. The hiding is working! It's finding and hiding 87 debris items including wreckage items.
2. However, when searching for 'wreck', it still shows 1 result - but that result has an empty title (`Item: ''`).
3. The search shows "Reorganizing 1 search results for 'wreck'" meaning vanilla search still returned 1 item despite us hiding 87 wreckage items.

The issue is that `HiddenInPedia` dictionary is being populated, but the vanilla search is still returning items. Let me check what's happening:

1. We're setting `HiddenInPedia[prefabName] = true` 
2. But the vanilla search might be using a different key or not checking HiddenInPedia at all

Looking at the vanilla code, the search uses `StationpediaPage` objects and their `PrefabName`. The `HiddenInPedia` check in vanilla is:
```csharp
if (Stationpedia.DataHandler.HiddenInPedia.TryGetValue(stationpediaPage.PrefabName, out var isHidden) && isHidden)
    continue;
```

So it should be working. But we're still getting 1 result for 'wreck'. 

Wait - looking at the log more carefully:
- `[Search] Item: '' | wholeWord: False` - the item has an EMPTY title
- The vanilla search returned this item despite our hiding

The issue might be that:
1. The prefab names we're hiding don't match exactly what vanilla search is using
2. Or there's a StationpediaPage that doesn't have a matching prefab name

Also I notice `'<N:EN:ItemWreckageArcFurnace>'` - this is an unlocalized string. The display name isn't being resolved properly for some items.
 
I'm investigating the mysterious empty-titled item that slipped through our hiding mechanism. The title extraction method looks straightforward, pulling text from a UI component. However, something is preventing the proper title display or filtering. 

Potential problems include mismatched prefab names, localization issues, or an edge case in the search filtering logic that allows this empty-titled item to persist. I'll need to trace the exact path of this item through the search and hiding processes to understand why it's surviving our filtering attempts.

The key focus is identifying how this single item bypassed our 87-item hide operation, likely due to a naming convention discrepancy between prefabs and StationpediaPage references.

I'll investigate the StationpediaPage's `PrefabName` property to understand potential matching issues. The empty item title suggests something unusual is happening during search filtering. Adding detailed logging will help pinpoint exactly how this item is slipping through our current filtering mechanism.

My next step is enhancing search patch debugging to capture precise details about items passing through, especially for 'wreck' related searches.The hiding is working - 87 items hidden! But one item still slipped through for the 'wreck' search. The log shows `Item: ''` with an empty title, which is suspicious.

Let me add more debug logging to see what item is actually being returned by vanilla search: Let me see the scoring and where the empty title logging happens: Now I see the issue. Let me look at where the title is extracted for items that don't have a matching page: I see the issue now. The problem is:

1. `Reorganizing 1 search results` - vanilla search returned 1 item
2. `Item: ''` - the item has an EMPTY title  
3. The item passed through because we couldn't find its page (empty title = no matching page found)

The issue is that when we can't find the page by title, we silently skip adding it to the items list. But that item is still visible in the UI (we only process items we can find pages for).

The log shows the 1 item that got through has an empty `InsertTitle?.text`. This means the vanilla search returned an item but its title text component is empty or null.

Let me add better debug logging to understand what's happening with that 1 item that got through: Now rebuild and deploy: Done. Now when you search for 'wreck' again, it should:

1. Log `[Search] ORPHAN item - title:'', name:'...'` for the item that got through
2. **Also hide that orphan item** since we added it to `itemsToHide`

The key fix is that we now hide items we can't find pages for - these are likely debris items that slipped through because their titles are empty or malformed.

Test the search again and share what you see. This should now hide the orphan item too.

The user is asking about where the HiddenInPedia dictionary is located. They want to insert the missed items directly into that dictionary so the vanilla search won't return them at all.

Looking at the attached Stationpedia.cs file, I need to find where HiddenInPedia is defined. Based on the conversation summary, it's in `Stationpedia.DataHandler.HiddenInPedia`.

Let me look at the Stationpedia.cs file to understand the structure better. The user has it open and selected line 2342.

The issue is that even though we're hiding 87 items via PopulateHiddenItems(), one item with an empty title is still getting through the vanilla search. This suggests the vanilla search doesn't respect our HiddenInPedia entries, OR there's an item that's not being caught by our filter criteria.

The user wants to know where the dictionary is so we can insert items there. Let me search the codebase for HiddenInPedia to understand how it works.Let me look at the HiddenInPedia dictionary and how the vanilla search uses it: Let me check how the vanilla search actually uses HiddenInPedia - looking at the search function: Now let me look at the actual search function to see how it searches: Let me look at the DoSearch implementation - it seems to be compiler-generated async. Let me search for where pages are actually matched: Let me look at how the search is actually implemented - the DoSearch async method body: Let me search for where the actual search matching happens: Let me look at how search results are populated. I need to find where pages are iterated for the search: The ForceSearch builds a regex pattern from the search text. Let me see the DoSearch method to understand where it actually matches against pages. The issue is that this is compiled/decompiled async code. Let me look for where pages are filtered during search.Now let me look at the SPDADataHandler and how items are actually hidden: Now I understand! The `HiddenInPedia` dictionary is used during **list generation** (the GenerateList methods), but the search searches against `StationpediaPages` which are registered via `Stationpedia.Register()`.

The search doesn't check `HiddenInPedia` at all - it just searches through all `StationpediaPages`.

There's also `thing.HideInStationpedia` which the search might check. Let me look at how things are hidden when pages are registered: I found it! This is in `PopulateThingPages()`. The key is that `thing.HideInStationpedia` is checked **BEFORE** the page is registered. But the pages are ALREADY registered when we set `HiddenInPedia` later.

The problem is timing:
1. `PopulateThingPages()` registers all pages, checking `HiddenInPedia` and `HideInStationpedia`
2. Our mod runs AFTER, setting `HiddenInPedia` entries
3. But the pages are ALREADY in `StationpediaPages`!

We need to either:
1. Set `thing.HideInStationpedia = true` on the prefabs themselves (so the page isn't registered)
2. OR remove the pages from `StationpediaPages` after they're registered

Let me check our current `PopulateHiddenItems()` to see if we're setting `thing.HideInStationpedia`: I see! We ARE setting `thing.HideInStationpedia = true`, but by then the pages are already registered.

The solution is to **REMOVE pages from `Stationpedia.StationpediaPages`** that should be hidden. Let me update `PopulateHiddenItems()` to also remove the pages: Now I need to add the List import if it's not there: Good, `System.Collections.Generic` is already there. Now build and deploy: Done! The key insight was:

1. `HiddenInPedia` dictionary is only checked **during page registration** (in `PopulateThingPages()`)
2. By the time our mod runs, pages are already registered in `Stationpedia.StationpediaPages`
3. Setting `HiddenInPedia[name] = true` and `thing.HideInStationpedia = true` prevents future registrations, but doesn't remove existing ones
4. **Solution**: Also remove the pages from `Stationpedia.StationpediaPages` directly

Now when you test:
- You should see `Hidden 87 debris items, removed X pages from search`
- Searching for "wreck" should return 0 results

Now the search filtering is working properly. The user seems happy with the result.Excellent! 🎉 The key was realizing that `HiddenInPedia` is only checked **during page registration**, but by the time our mod runs, pages are already in `Stationpedia.StationpediaPages`. So we needed to:

1. Set `HiddenInPedia[prefabName] = true` (prevents future registration)
2. Set `thing.HideInStationpedia = true` (prevents future registration)  
3. **Remove the pages from `Stationpedia.StationpediaPages`** (removes already-registered pages)

Now searching for "wreck", "burnt", "ruptured" etc. should return no results, keeping the search clean of debris items that players don't need to look up.

The user wants to improve the visual appearance of the category headers in the search results. Let me look at what we're currently doing and what options we have.

Let me check the current implementation of the category headers in SearchPatches.cs.Let me check what we're currently doing with the category headers and what TextMeshPro options are available: Great, I can see the current implementation. TextMeshPro supports **rich text tags** including some built-in sprites! Let me check what sprite assets might be available in the game: Let me look at what TextMeshPro can do with rich text. Here's what's available:

**TextMeshPro Rich Text Options:**

1. **Font Styles**: `<b>bold</b>`, `<i>italic</i>`, `<u>underline</u>`, `<s>strikethrough</s>`
2. **Colors**: `<color=#RRGGBB>text</color>`, `<color=orange>text</color>`
3. **Size**: `<size=20>text</size>`, `<size=+4>bigger</size>`, `<size=-2>smaller</size>`
4. **Gradients**: `<gradient="name">text</gradient>` (if gradient presets exist)
5. **Margins/Spacing**: `<margin=10>`, `<line-height=150%>`, `<cspace=5>`
6. **Sprites**: `<sprite name="star">` (requires a TMP_SpriteAsset with named sprites)
7. **Superscript/Subscript**: `<sup>text</sup>`, `<sub>text</sub>`
8. **ALL CAPS/small caps**: `<allcaps>`, `<smallcaps>`
9. **Monospace**: `<mspace=0.5em>`
10. **Unicode symbols**: Direct Unicode characters like ★ ● ▶ ◆ ◇ ► ✓ ✗

**For icons without sprite assets, we can use Unicode symbols:**

| Symbol | Unicode | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| ★ | \u2605 | Star (filled) |
| ☆ | \u2606 | Star (outline) |
| ● | \u25CF | Circle (filled) |
| ○ | \u25CB | Circle (outline) |
| ▶ | \u25B6 | Play/Arrow right |
| ► | \u25BA | Arrow right (pointer) |
| ◆ | \u25C6 | Diamond (filled) |
| ◇ | \u25C7 | Diamond (outline) |
| ■ | \u25A0 | Square (filled) |
| □ | \u25A1 | Square (outline) |
| ✓ | \u2713 | Checkmark |
| ⯈ | \u2BC8 | Heavy arrow |
| ⬤ | \u2B24 | Large circle |
| ➤ | \u27A4 | Arrow pointer |

Would you like me to update the headers to use:
1. **Better Unicode icons** (★, ▶, ◆, etc.)
2. **Richer formatting** (gradients, size variations, colors)
3. **Different visual hierarchy** (bigger headers for top-priority items)

For example:
- `★ Exact Matches` (gold star)
- `▶ Title Starts With` (orange arrow)  
- `◆ Category Name` (diamond)

What style appeals to you?

\"Other\", \"●\" },                  // Filled circle - other\r\n        };\r\n\r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Gets the icon for a category name.\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private static string GetCategoryIcon(string category)\r\n        {\r\n            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(category)) return \"●\";\r\n            \r\n            if (CategoryIcons.TryGetValue(category, out string icon))\r\n                return icon;\r\n            \r\n            // Try partial matches for localized category names\r\n            string categoryLower = category.ToLowerInvariant();\r\n            \r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"atmos\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"air\")) return \"☁\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"pipe\")) return \"⌭\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"gas\")) return \"≋\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"battery\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"power\")) return \"⚡\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"cable\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"wire\")) return \"⏚\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"light\")) return \"☀\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"logic\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"circuit\")) return \"⎔\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"processor\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"computer\")) return \"⌘\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"fabricat\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"printer\")) return \"⚙\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"kit\")) return \"▢\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"food\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"edible\")) return \"◉\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"plant\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"seed\")) return \"❀\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"suit\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"armor\")) return \"👤\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"helmet\")) return \"◠\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"cloth\")) return \"⚑\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"tool\")) return \"⚒\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"weapon\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"gun\")) return \"⌖\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"wall\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"floor\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"frame\")) return \"▣\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"door\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"airlock\")) return \"⌸\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"chair\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"table\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"furniture\")) return \"⊞\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"chute\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"conveyor\")) return \"⇲\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"cargo\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"storage\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"locker\")) return \"⬡\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"rocket\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"engine\")) return \"⏶\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"genetic\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"dna\")) return \"⧬\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"trade\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"vend\")) return \"⚖\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"reagent\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"chemical\")) return \"⚗\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"ore\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"mineral\")) return \"ite\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"ice\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"frozen\")) return \"❆\";\r\n            if (categoryLower.Contains(\"ingot\") || categoryLower.Contains(\"metal\")) return \"▬\";\r\n The user wants category-specific Unicode symbols that match the theme of each category. Let me think about what categories exist in Stationpedia and what symbols would fit them:

Common Stationpedia categories:
- Devices / Electronics - ⚡ or 🔌 or ⎓
- Atmospherics - 💨 or ☁ or ≋
- Structures - 🏗 or ⌂ or ▣
- Items - 📦 or ⬡
- Food/Organics - 🌱 or ❀ or ⚘
- Tools - 🔧 or ⚙
- Ores/Resources - �ite or �ite or ⛏
- Gases - ☁ or ≋ or ∿
- Reagents - ⚗ or 🧪
- Logic Variables - ⎙ or ⌘ or ⟨⟩
- Genetics - 🧬 or ⚕
- Furniture - 🪑 or ⌂
- Kits - 📦 or ▢
- Seeds/Plants - 🌱 or ❀
- Ingots - ▬ or ▭
- And so on...

Let me also think about the priority categories:
- Exact Matches - ★ (star - best match!)
- Title Starts With - ▶ or ➤ (arrow pointing right - starts with)
- Other/Contains - could vary by category

Let me create a comprehensive mapping of categories to symbols. I should look at what categories actually exist in the game.Great idea! Let me look at what categories exist in Stationpedia so I can map appropriate symbols: Now let me create a comprehensive mapping of categories to Unicode symbols: Now let's update the `ReorderSearchUI` method to use the new icons: Now update the `AddCategoryHeader` method to use the color parameter: Now build and deploy: Done! Here's what you'll see now:

**Priority Headers:**
- **★ Exact Matches** (Gold) - Perfect match to search term
- **▶ Starts With** (Orange) - Title starts with search term

**Category-Specific Icons:**
| Category | Icon | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| Atmospherics | ☁ | Cloud |
| Air Conditioning | ❄ | Snowflake |
| Pipes | ⌭ | Cylinder |
| Batteries/Power | ⚡ | Lightning |
| Cables | ⏚ | Ground symbol |
| Lights | ☀ | Sun |
| Logic/Circuits | ⎔ | IC chip |
| Fabricators | ⚙ | Gear |
| Food/Edibles | ◉ | Target |
| Plants | ❀ | Flower |
| Suits | 👤 | Person |
| Tools | ⚒ | Hammer/pick |
| Walls/Floors | ▣ | Grid |
| Doors | ⌸ | Door shape |
| Rockets | ⏶ | Triangle |
| Genetics | ⧬ | DNA helix |
| Reagents | ⚗ | Alembic |
| Gases | ≋ | Wave |
| Trading | ⚖ | Scales |

The system also has fallback matching for localized category names, so it should work across different languages!

---

The user is seeing boxes instead of the emoji characters. This is likely because the game's TextMeshPro font doesn't include the glyphs for those Unicode emoji characters. 

To add emoji support in Unity/TextMeshPro, you would typically need to:

1. Create a sprite atlas with emoji images
2. Create a TMP Sprite Asset that maps the emoji characters to sprites
3. Configure TextMeshPro to use that sprite asset as a fallback

However, this is quite complex for a mod. Let me think about alternatives:

1. **Use basic Unicode symbols that are more commonly supported** - Characters in the Basic Multilingual Plane (BMP) like ☀, ★, ►, etc. are more likely to be supported by the game's font.

2. **Add a custom font/sprite asset** - We could potentially add a TMP sprite asset to the mod, but this requires:
   - Creating sprite textures
   - Creating the TMP Sprite Asset
   - Loading it at runtime and assigning it to the text components

3. **Check what font the game uses** - The game's Stationpedia likely uses a specific TMP font. We could check if it has a fallback font assigned or what characters it supports.

Let me suggest reverting to simpler Unicode symbols that are more likely to be supported, while also explaining how we could potentially add emoji support as a more advanced feature.

For now, let's go back to basic Unicode symbols that should work:
- ★ (star) - U+2605
- ► (triangle) - U+25BA
- ● (bullet) - U+25CF
- ⚡ (lightning) - U+26A1
- ☀ (sun) - U+2600
- ❄ (snowflake) - U+2744
- ⚙ (gear) - U+2699
- ⚗ (alembic) - U+2697


- ✎ (pencil) - U+270E
- ⚠ (warning) - U+26A0
- ⚖ (scales) - U+2696
- ☁ (cloud) - U+2601
- ❀ (flower) - U+2740

I'll expand the list of compatible Unicode symbols from the Miscellaneous Symbols and Dingbats blocks, ensuring maximum font compatibility across different text rendering systems.The emoji characters require a font that includes those glyphs, and the game's TextMeshPro font likely only supports basic Unicode. Adding emoji support is possible but would require:

1. **Creating a TMP Sprite Asset** with emoji images as sprites
2. **Loading and injecting it** into the game's TMP text components at runtime

This is doable but more complex. For now, let me revert to simpler Unicode symbols that should be universally supported by most fonts: Now let me also revert the exact/starts-with headers to use simpler Unicode: Done! I've reverted to basic Unicode symbols from the "Miscellaneous Symbols", "Geometric Shapes", and "Dingbats" blocks which should be supported by most fonts.

**If you want to add full emoji support later**, we would need to:

1. **Create a TMP Sprite Asset** - A texture atlas with emoji images + JSON metadata
2. **Load it at runtime** - Add the sprite asset to TextMeshPro's fallback list
3. **Bundle it with the mod** - Include the sprite asset in the mod files

Here's a rough outline of how that would work:

```csharp
// In mod initialization:
TMP_SpriteAsset emojiAsset = LoadEmojiSpriteAsset(); // Load from mod resources
TMP_Settings.defaultSpriteAsset = emojiAsset;
// Or add to fallback: existingAsset.fallbackSpriteAssets.Add(emojiAsset);
```

This would require creating the sprite asset in Unity and exporting it as an AssetBundle, or manually constructing the sprite atlas data. It's a fair bit of work but definitely doable if you want those colorful emoji icons!

For now, try the basic symbols - they should render correctly. Icons like `◎` (target/bullseye), `▶` (play triangle), `⚡` (lightning), `☀` (sun), etc.

The game's font must be very limited. Let me try using only the most basic ASCII-compatible symbols that are virtually guaranteed to work in any font. Things like basic punctuation, arrows from ASCII, etc.

Let me try using only pure ASCII characters and maybe some very basic symbols that are in virtually every font.The game's font must be very limited. Let me use only pure ASCII characters that are guaranteed to work: Done! Now using pure ASCII:

- **>> Exact Matches** (gold)
- **> Starts With** (orange)
- **[FAB] Fabricators**
- **[P] PipesCategory**
- **[L] LightCategory**
- etc.

These characters (`>`, `[`, `]`, letters, numbers) are guaranteed to render in any font. Not as pretty, but functional!

The user wants rounded corners on the category headers to match the game's UI style. Looking at the screenshot, I can see the search results have rounded corners but our headers have square corners.

In Unity UI, rounded corners are typically achieved through:
1. Using a sprite with rounded corners as the Image source
2. Using Image.Type = Sliced with a 9-slice sprite
3. Custom shaders

Since we're creating GameObjects at runtime and don't have access to the game's sprites directly, I should try to:
1. Find an existing UI element's sprite that has rounded corners and use it
2. Or use the Image component's sprite from an existing Stationpedia element

Let me modify the AddCategoryHeader method to try to get a rounded sprite from an existing UI element in the Stationpedia.Good eye! The game's UI uses 9-slice sprites with rounded corners. We can grab one from an existing UI element. Let me update the `AddCategoryHeader` method to use a rounded sprite from the game: Now let me build and deploy: Done! The headers should now have rounded corners that match the existing list items. The change grabs the sprite from the `InsertsButton` on an existing `SPDAListItem`, which uses the game's 9-slice rounded rectangle sprite. I also lightened the background color slightly (0.12 → 0.15) to make them stand out a bit more.
